batch_summary.note: Jul 2025: 1 primary RF-09 signal. S008 Sev-2 Level A — 'There's no threats' denial of documented threat ROW-9957. XREF-001 created.

primary_flags.cross_rf: RF-05 (S020 DARVO-denial mechanism B-027 ROW-9964); RF-04 (DARVO cluster)
primary_flags.message_text: ROW-9957: 'I'll screenshot this and file for full custody' | ROW-9960: 'Call me crazy if you want to but that is what will happen' | ROW-9964: 'There's no threats'
primary_flags.summary: Jul 19 2025: The clearest Level A cross-reference pair in the dataset. Within the same message cluster, Melissa issues an explicit legal custody threat then denies it. Sequence: ROW-9957 'I'll screenshot this and file for full custody' — direct documented legal threat; ROW-9960 'Call me crazy if you want to but that is what will happen' — threat reinforcement; ROW-9964 'There's no threats' — explicit denial of the threat made 7 messages earlier in the same cluster. This is Level A (direct textual contradiction): the denial and the denied statement are both in the text record, in the same batch, and require no corroboration beyond the messages themselves. XREF-001: crossref-index.json entry created. Sev-2 per ADD-001 (Level A = Sev-2 minimum). ADD-003 compound: DARVO sequence containing explicit reality-denial → RF-09+RF-04 primary. RF-05 compound: S020 (RF-05 pass) documented ROW-9964 as DARVO-denial mechanism — this is the same event; compound flag COMP-RF09-005 covers RF-09+RF-05. The denial 'There's no threats' is the most efficiently documentable gaslighting event in the dataset: anchor message and denial message are both in the same message cluster with no ambiguity.

crossref_unresolved.resolution: Both anchor and contradiction are in the same cluster; no forward search required.

arc_updates.event: S008 signal added; first Level A cross-reference pair confirmed (XREF-001)
